terms.definition: Systematic error of a learner: gap between the expected prediction E[ŷ] and the true target f*(x). Canonical definition for bias in the bias–variance decomposition in this topic.
Squared bias term (E[ŷ] − f*(x))² in the expected squared-error decomposition; always non-negative and averaged or integrated over x in practice.
Large systematic miss — model too simple or mis-specified; associated with underfitting and high training error.
Model fails to capture trainable structure; practical symptom of high bias (both train and validation error remain high).
Set of functions a learning algorithm can represent (e.g. all lines, all depth-3 trees). A restrictive class imposes inductive bias and can raise estimation bias.
Assumptions built into a model (linearity, smoothness, sparsity). Helpful when correct; source of high bias when wrong for the data.
